introPage.title: JOMAP
introPage.subtitle: A community based locations and activities
introPage.description: A unified digital community platform dedicated and tailored to the Jordanian context. The platform integrates interactive geographical services with activities and events management, enhanced by a collaborative social environment that hosts two main types of entities: profit-seeking commercial businesses that benefit from an innovative promotional system offering exclusive and attractive discounts, and non-profit volunteer teams and organizations that organize community initiatives and events. This actively contributes to stimulating the local economy, supporting youth and volunteer work, and creates a seamless link connecting location discovery, activity participation, and exclusive offers.
introPage.simpleDescription:
JO MAP is a platform that brings all locations, events, and activities happening in Jordan into one place.

Many exciting activities, offers, restaurants, or unique spots in the country struggle to reach people. At the same time, people want to go out and have fun but don't know where to go, simply because there is no real platform connecting them together.

Here comes the idea of JO MAP: the app immediately connects you to your current area, focusing on all nearby offers, events, and locations.

We didn't build maps from scratch or waste time reinventing the wheel; we integrated Google Maps, leveraged its powerful data, and built this interactive system on top. This is the true role of software engineering: combining existing solutions to deliver a real, useful product that serves the user, the business owner, and strengthens the Jordanian community.
introPage.rolesTitle: The Three Main Roles in JOMAP Ecosystem

user.title: The Explorer / Regular User
user.subtitle: Mobile App User
user.desc: Represents the wide audience of youth, families, and tourists interested in exploring Jordan and community interaction.
user.actions: Explores interactive maps, joins events, redeems local offers, posts daily stories, and uses Nashmi AI to plan trips.

owner.title: Business Owner / Entity
owner.subtitle: Mobile Business Panel
owner.desc: Represents local businesses (restaurants, cafes, shops) and non-profit youth/volunteer event organizers.
owner.actions: Registers business on maps, launches geolocated promotional offers, manages events, and tracks performance on mobile.

admin.title: Platform Administrator (Admin)
admin.subtitle: Web Control Dashboard
admin.desc: Represents the regulatory and monitoring entity ensuring safety, reliability, and quality of platform content.
admin.actions: Moderates the ecosystem via Web Admin Dashboard, approves locations, reviews reports, and monitors global metrics.

introPage.pillarsTitle: The Four Pillars of the Project

community.title: Social Network
community.desc: Sharing moments and experiences to build genuine interaction

maps.title: Interactive Locations
maps.desc: Smart geographical exploration of landmarks and services in Jordan

events.title: Events & Activities
events.desc: Organizing and participating in local and youth initiatives

offers.title: Offers & Discounts
offers.desc: A smart promotional system targeting users geographically

p1.title: Absence of Unified Platforms
p1.desc: Lack of a single application that integrates activities, events, exclusive offers, and interactive maps in one place.

p2.title: Geographic Access Difficulty
p2.desc: Difficult for local business owners and projects to reach their target audience geographically.

p3.title: Lack of Community Interaction
p3.desc: Absence of an active social and participatory environment that connects individuals with their local community.

p4.title: Content Credibility Challenges
p4.desc: Difficult to moderate content and verify offers and locations without a unified authority.

p5.title: Challenges in Volunteer Work
p5.desc: Difficulty organizing and promoting youth initiatives and volunteer work due to the lack of a centralized platform connecting non-profit teams with local volunteers.

p6.title: Limited Modern Tech & AI Integration
p6.desc: Limited integration of modern technologies (like real-time geofencing and AI recommendation engines) to smart-guide users to personalized experiences.

o1.title: Unified Integrated Hub
o1.desc: Integrating maps, events, and offers with a community network in a single application.

o2.title: Boost Tourism & Social Work
o2.desc: Promoting local tourism, family activities, and supporting youth volunteer initiatives.

o3.title: Support Local Economy
o3.desc: Enabling merchants to promote their offers with smart geolocated targeting to attract customers.

o4.title: Ensure Quality & Trust
o4.desc: Providing an admin moderation dashboard to verify the credibility of locations and offers.

o5.title: Activate Youth & Volunteer Work
o5.desc: Enabling and promoting the culture of volunteerism and youth initiatives by providing tools that make it easy for community teams to organize and promote their activities directly to interested local audiences.

o6.title: Strengthening Jordanian Community Bonds
o6.desc: Creating a digital link that supports local initiatives and connects members of the Jordanian community, fostering social cohesion and positive interaction across all governorates.

buildPage.title: How JoMap Was Engineered
buildPage.subtitle: Built from scratch following global Software Engineering principles and the full SDLC across two academic graduation projects
buildPage.intro: JoMap was engineered from zero following rigorous Software Engineering standards. The entire development lifecycle — from brainstorming to deployment — was documented and tracked using Jira and GitHub across Graduation Project 1 (Analysis & Architecture) and Graduation Project 2 (Implementation & Testing).

phase1.title: Problem Analysis & Ideation
phase1.text: Started by brainstorming real problems facing Jordan's tourism and local community discovery. Analyzed the gap: no single platform combines interactive maps, social networking, and event booking. Defined the project scope to serve 3 user roles (Citizens, Business Owners, Administrators) within one unified ecosystem.

phase2.title: Requirements Engineering
phase2.text: Conducted deep requirements gathering: functional requirements (28+ screens, 50+ API endpoints), non-functional requirements (performance, security, scalability), and stakeholder analysis. Defined system boundaries, use cases, and user stories. Prioritized features using MoSCoW methodology.

phase3.title: System Design & UML Modeling
phase3.text: Created comprehensive engineering diagrams following SE standards: Class Diagrams (12+ entities with complex relations), Component Diagrams, State Diagrams (multi-state lifecycles for locations, bookings, moderation), Sequence Diagrams, and Activity Diagrams. All versioned and documented on Jira.

phase4.title: Architecture & Documentation
phase4.text: Designed the full system architecture: 3-tier client-server with REST APIs, JWT authentication, PostgreSQL for geospatial data, Firebase for cloud storage. Culminated in an 84-page final documentation covering every technical specification, approved as the blueprint for implementation.

phase5.title: Backend Development
phase5.text: Implemented the backend using Spring Boot with 10 controllers, 12+ services, and 50+ REST API endpoints. Built 12+ JPA entities with complex relationships. Integrated PostgreSQL (Neon Serverless) for geolocation coordinates, JWT security, Firebase Storage for images, and Spring Mail for email services.

phase6.title: Mobile App Development
phase6.text: Built the Android app using Kotlin following MVVM architecture: 14+ packages, 28+ fragments/screens, 10+ ViewModels, 15+ adapters. Integrated Google Maps SDK, Firebase Cloud Messaging for push notifications, Retrofit for API communication, Gemini AI for an intelligent chatbot, and QR code scanning for tickets.

phase7.title: Testing & Integration
phase7.text: Conducted comprehensive testing: unit tests for backend services, API endpoint testing via Postman (50+ endpoints validated), UI testing on multiple Android devices, integration testing for Maps + Backend + Firebase pipeline. Verified all 3 user role flows (Citizen, Owner with dashboard, Admin with moderation panel).

openApp.text: The user opens the mobile app and begins the exploration journey.

login.title: Login / Create Account
login.text: The user creates an account or logs in to access personalized features.

explore.title: Explore Jordan's Map
explore.text: The user browses Jordan's governorates using the interactive map.

search.title: Search for Places
search.text: The user can search for tourist sites, restaurants, hotels, or markets.

selectLocation.title: Select Location
selectLocation.text: The user taps on a location marker or card to open place details.

viewDetails.title: View Details
viewDetails.text: The app displays photos, descriptions, active offers, and community events.

saveRate.title: Save to Favorites
saveRate.text: The user saves their favorite places and activities for future reference.

contactOwner.title: Direct Communication
contactOwner.text: The user can contact the place owner or event organizer directly for inquiries.

complete.title: Experience Complete
complete.text: The user finishes their journey with an easier and smarter way to discover Jordan.

ownerFlowPage.title: Business Dashboard Flow
ownerFlowPage.subtitle: This flow shows the steps business owners take from account registration and document verification, to managing locations and publishing promotional offers and events.

login.title: Owner Portal Login
login.text: Log in to the owner portal to access management and promotional tools.

registerBusiness.title: Submit Business Data
registerBusiness.text: Provide business name, registration files, location details, and contact info.

waitApproval.title: System Review
waitApproval.text: The administration reviews the submission to ensure trust and content quality.

addLocation.title: Set / Edit Location
addLocation.text: Pin coordinates on the interactive map and write details, working hours, and media.

createOffer.title: Publish Offers & Discounts
createOffer.text: Create geolocated promotional discounts to attract nearby explorers.

addEvent.title: Organize Events & Activities
addEvent.text: Plan and launch community initiatives or commercial activities to drive engagement.

viewAnalytics.title: Track Analytics
viewAnalytics.text: Monitor views, user engagement on offers, ratings, and customer reviews.

adminFlowPage.title: Moderation & System Management
adminFlowPage.subtitle: This flow illustrates how system administrators review business credentials, moderate user-generated content, and monitor analytics to keep the platform safe.

login.title: Admin Dashboard Login
login.text: Securely log into the web administration panel.

reviewReq.title: Verify Business Requests
reviewReq.text: Audit document submissions, verification documents, and check maps updates.

approveReq.title: Approve & Publish Locations
approveReq.text: Approve listings to display them and their promotional offers on the public maps.

moderateContent.title: Content Moderation
moderateContent.text: Review user flags, report logs, block inappropriate content or fake reviews.

viewGlobalMetrics.title: Monitor System Health
viewGlobalMetrics.text: View platform statistics, daily active users, volume of offers, and database status.

screensPage.title: Application Screens
screensPage.subtitle: These screens show the main user experience within JoMap, from opening the app to exploring places and managing the user account.

home.title: Home Screen

map.text: Users can explore Jordan using an interactive map with location markers.

solutionsPage.title: Technical Solutions
solutionsPage.subtitle: Complex Problems We Faced and How We Solved Them

notifications.problem: Problem: Notification Congestion Between User Types
notifications.problemText: How can we deliver social notifications to regular users and business notifications (ratings, offers) to owners without overlap or disturbance?
notifications.solutionText: Building completely separate screens and databases (OwnerNotificationScreen). Notifications are routed based on user role (Role) and each notification is linked to a deep link that takes the user to the required action immediately.

mapPerformance.problem: Problem: Map Performance with Many Locations and Offers
mapPerformance.problemText: Loading thousands of markers on the map at once causes severe lag on the phone interface and battery consumption.
mapPerformance.solutionText: Using Marker Clustering technology to group nearby locations into a single expandable icon when zooming, while fetching data based on current screen coordinates only (Bounding Box) through Spring Boot.

imageSpeed.problem: Problem: Image Loading Speed for Places and Users
imageSpeed.problemText: Loading high-resolution images for hundreds of places and reviews consumes user data and increases waiting time.
imageSpeed.solution: Solution: Optimized Cloud Storage (Firebase Storage & Caching)
imageSpeed.solutionText: Using Firebase Storage for image uploads, relying on caching libraries in Jetpack Compose like Coil to ensure images are loaded only once and saved locally, with image compression before uploading from the backend.

futureWorkPage.title: Future Work & Conclusion
futureWorkPage.subtitle: Next steps in developing JOMAP and expanding its services to better serve the community and boost tourism.

g1.title: AI Recommendation Engine
g1.desc: Build a smart system using generative AI models to automatically design personalized itineraries for users.

g2.title: Regional Expansion
g2.desc: Expand the service model to include major Arab cities and regional tourist destinations.

g3.title: Booking & Payment Integration
g3.desc: Integrate electronic payment gateways to book event tickets, restaurant tables, and activities directly within the app.

g4.title: Augmented Reality (AR) Tours
g4.desc: Provide visual and interactive guided tours inside Jordanian archeological sites like Petra and Jerash.

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.text: The JOMAP project represents an integrated solution that bridges ready-made technologies with the needs of the Jordanian community, achieving a unique balance between location discovery, supporting local businesses, and activating youth volunteering to be a true digital companion in every governorate.

techPage.backendDesc: Spring Boot provides REST APIs, authentication, business logic, database connection, and email sending service.
techPage.databaseDesc: The system stores user data, places, ratings, favorites, reports, and owner location data.
